description: Cross-agent handoff definitions for proactive operations. Agents read this at runtime from .handoff-protocol.json in their workspace.

handoffs.trigger: Kit resolves a complex or recurring bug
handoffs.payload: Bug description, root cause, fix approach, prevention strategy
handoffs.action: Scribe creates a knowledge article documenting the bug pattern

handoffs.trigger: Trak completes a sprint summary or retrospective
handoffs.payload: Sprint metrics, velocity, blockers, team feedback
handoffs.action: Scribe captures sprint insights in documentation

handoffs.trigger: Scout resolves a customer issue that reveals a common pattern
handoffs.payload: Issue description, resolution steps, frequency data
handoffs.action: Scribe creates/updates FAQ or troubleshooting guide

handoffs.trigger: Scout identifies repeated customer feature requests
handoffs.payload: Feature description, customer count, business impact estimate
handoffs.action: Trak creates a Jira feature request with customer evidence

handoffs.trigger: Kit identifies significant technical debt during code review
handoffs.payload: Debt description, affected components, estimated effort, risk level
handoffs.action: Trak creates a tech debt Jira issue with proper prioritization

handoffs.trigger: Trak detects a PR that's been open and idle for 48+ hours
handoffs.payload: PR number, author, reviewer status, blocking reason if known
handoffs.action: Kit pings reviewers and offers to help unblock

handoffs.trigger: Scribe detects documentation that's 90+ days stale and references active systems
handoffs.payload: Document link, last updated date, systems referenced, suggested owner
handoffs.action: Notify the relevant agent to review and update their domain docs

handoffs.trigger: Scout receives multiple customer reports about the same bug
handoffs.payload: Bug description, affected customers count, reproduction steps, ticket links
handoffs.action: Kit investigates the bug and creates a GitHub issue if confirmed

handoffs.trigger: A deployment completes (tag pushed, deploy hook fires)
handoffs.payload: Deploy tag, environment URL, list of merged PRs
handoffs.action: Probe runs post-deploy smoke tests against critical user flows

handoffs.trigger: A Jira bug ticket has reproduction steps and needs verification
handoffs.payload: Jira ticket ID, reproduction steps, environment details
handoffs.action: Probe attempts browser-based reproduction and attaches evidence to ticket

handoffs.trigger: Probe successfully reproduces a reported bug
handoffs.payload: Jira ticket ID, screenshots, console logs, exact reproduction steps
handoffs.action: Kit investigates the root cause and creates a fix

handoffs.payload: Pass/fail summary, deploy tag, test execution report
handoffs.action: Trak updates the deployment tracking record with test results

handoffs.trigger: Probe identifies missing test specifications or undocumented flows
handoffs.payload: Missing test specs, undocumented user flows, coverage gaps
handoffs.action: Scribe creates documentation for the missing test areas

handoffs.trigger: Kit creates an auto-fix PR that needs tracking
handoffs.payload: PR number, fix type (lint, dependency, security), affected components
handoffs.action: Trak adds the auto-fix PR to sprint tracking and ensures review assignment

handoffs.trigger: Scribe's knowledge gap analysis identifies undocumented features
handoffs.payload: List of documentation gaps with severity, suggested owners, Jira task IDs
handoffs.action: Trak prioritizes documentation tasks within sprint planning

protocol.delivery: Primary: sessions_send to the target agent's active session (requires tools.sessions.visibility=all in gateway config). Fallback: @mention the target agent in #dev channel with the full handoff payload. Bot-to-bot Slack DMs are NOT supported by Slack's API.
protocol.format: Include handoff ID, trigger context, and payload. Use thread for follow-up.
protocol.acknowledgment: Target agent must acknowledge within the conversation
protocol.timeout: If no acknowledgment in 30 minutes, retry once. After second failure, log and notify admin.
protocol.target_format: Single agent: string (e.g. "scribe"). Multiple agents: array (e.g. ["kit", "trak", "scout"]).

authentication.format: Include `[HMAC:<hex-signature>]` at the end of the handoff message. Signature is HMAC-SHA256 of the message body (everything before the HMAC tag) using the shared key from HANDOFF_HMAC_KEY environment variable.
authentication.verification: Receiving agent must verify the HMAC signature before processing the handoff. Reject messages with missing or invalid signatures.

primary.description: Send handoff message directly to the target agent's session via OpenClaw's internal session messaging. This is the fastest and most reliable method.
primary.how: Use the sessions_send tool with target agent:TARGET_NAME:main and include the HMAC-signed handoff payload.
primary.requires: tools.sessions.visibility must be set to 'all' in gateway config

fallback.description: Post handoff message to #dev (C086N5031LZ) with @mention of target agent. Used when sessions_send fails or target has no active session.
fallback.how: Post to #dev channel with @TARGET_USER_ID mention and full handoff payload including HMAC signature.

blocked.description: Bot-to-bot Slack DMs are NOT supported. Slack returns 'cannot_dm_bot'. Do NOT attempt this.
